title: Urinary retention

examination.text: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped medicine history, urinalysis, and adult male initial-assessment context.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.

investigations.text: Supports acute or chronic urinary-retention context, palpable or percussable bladder, residual volume, creatinine, qualified imaging, and clinician-led follow-up or referral; catheter and medicine instructions are not mapped.

source_limitations: The workflow does not encode adult male eligibility, acute versus chronic retention, neurological cause, renal impairment, hydronephrosis, or catheter status qualifiers.

Fever, haematuria, suprapubic discomfort, neurological symptoms or examination, trauma or surgery, pain severity, pelvic examination, post-renal acute kidney injury, cauda equina, catheter details, trial without catheter, generic clinician plan, safety-netting, fixed intervals, PRN wording, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
Cause, diagnosis, emergency neurological assessment, catheterization, medicine or procedure selection, referral urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
